response.justification: While this is a capable staff-level engineer with TypeScript and cloud infrastructure expertise who recently entered AI work, the candidate fundamentally does not meet the role's core requirement: 8+ years building and shipping production-grade AI solutions. Current GenAI exposure is limited to infrastructure tooling (started April 2025), and critical competencies—Python, vector databases, RAG architectures, data pipelines, and LLM optimization—are absent from the resume. The career trajectory has been primarily web/backend engineering, not AI-driven systems.
strengths: Staff-level engineer with proven technical depth across multiple languages and platforms, demonstrating strong architectural thinking
Currently embedded in AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq with exposure to MCP code generators and agentic workflows
Solid cloud experience (AWS CDK, Docker, CI/CD pipelines) and modern tech stack (TypeScript, Node.js) applicable to production deployment
concerns: Does not meet the fundamental 8+ year requirement for production AI solutions; only ~1-2 months of GenAI exposure in current role
No Python experience mentioned despite it being essential for AI/ML development and explicitly required for the position
No demonstrated expertise in vector databases, RAG architectures, data pipeline engineering, or LLM performance optimization—all core job responsibilities

key_factors.factor: Production AI systems experience (8+ years required)
Staff-level engineering capability with current AI infrastructure exposure
